title: Dog Swallowed Alkaline Battery: Emergency Guide & Next Steps
meta_desc: If your dog swallowed a battery, it is a high-priority emergency. Learn the risks of corrosive burns and why you must see a vet immediately.
content_html:
What Happens When a Dog Swallows a Battery?

Alkaline batteries contain corrosive chemicals such as potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide. When a dog bites or swallows a battery, the casing can leak, leading to liquefactive necrosis. This process causes deep, painful chemical burns that can dissolve tissues in the mouth, esophagus, and gastrointestinal tract.

Is This an Emergency? (Urgency Level: High)

Yes, this is a critical medical emergency. If your dog has swallowed or chewed an alkaline battery, you must transport them to an emergency veterinary clinic immediately. Internal damage can occur within minutes, and delayed treatment can lead to life-threatening complications such as esophageal perforation or heavy metal poisoning. Do not wait for symptoms to appear before seeking help.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If it is safe to do so, take a photo of the package, the device the battery came from, or a matching battery of the same brand. Providing this image to your veterinarian helps them identify the exact size, chemical composition, and potential for the battery to be a button/disc type, which carries an additional risk of electrical burns. Knowing the dimensions helps the vet decide if the battery is likely to pass or if surgical removal is necessary.

faqs.question: Should I induce vomiting at home?
faqs.answer: No. Never induce vomiting if your dog swallowed a battery. If the battery is leaking, the corrosive liquid will cause severe chemical burns to the esophagus as it is brought back up.

faqs.question: What are the symptoms of battery ingestion?
faqs.answer: Symptoms include excessive drooling, oral sores, black or tarry stools, abdominal pain, vomiting, and a refusal to eat. However, internal damage can occur before any outward signs are visible.

faqs.question: Can I give my dog milk or water?
faqs.answer: Do not give any food or liquids until you have spoken with a veterinarian. They will provide specific instructions based on how long ago the ingestion occurred.
